title: 'play kube' ignores cache + erroneous attempt to call non-existent api
body:
/kind bug

**Description**
Podman `play kube` incorrectly & inconsistently forces api call instead of first looking for images in local cache. This becomes an issue when attempting to run a pod from images loaded to the local cache as a means of solving many critical delivery problems.

Incorrect behavior:
  Executing `podman play kube docker.io/library/registry:latest` triggers an image source registry api lookup.
  Executing `podman play kube localhost/library/registry:latest` triggers an image source registry api lookup against `https://localhost/v2/`

Inconsistent behavior:
  1. The pod infra image `k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2` does not cause the erroneous source registry api lookup
  2. Executing `podman run` cmds does not cause the erroneous image source registry api lookup
  3. Expected behavior is visible in podman 1.6.4 (no failure or error produced, images successfully loaded from local cache)

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Prep pod yaml & image cache for offline test:
```
# Create Pod
podman pod create --replace \
    --name cloudctl \
    --hostname cloudctl \
    --publish 5000:5000/tcp \
    --dns-search codectl.io \
  --infra-image k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2

# Create container in pod
podman run -d --pod cloudctl --name registry \
    --volume /root/deploy/mirror:/root/deploy/mirror:z \
  docker.io/library/registry:latest

# Generate pod yaml definition
podman generate kube cloudctl | tee /tmp/cloudctl.yml

# Remove pod
podman rm --force cloudctl

# Confirm images are cached
podman images
```

2. Set practical disconnect from internet access & test pod
```
Confirm all default routes are deleted
ip r del default
ip r

# Run `podman play kube` command to start pod
podman play kube /tmp/cloudctl.yml

# Observe pod `pause` container starts without issue 
# Observe defined container image start error message & failure

# Remove pod
podman pod rm --force cloudctl
```

3. Execute control tests
```
## Control Test #1 
# Re-tag & call pod imag(es) from `localhost` to imply pull from cache
podman tag docker.io/library/registry:latest library/registry:latest
sed -i 's/docker.io/localhost/g' /tmp/cloudctl.yml

# Re-run podman play kube
podman play kube /tmp/cloudctl.yml

# Observe repeat of errors including attempt to call docker registry api from `localhost`

## Control Test #2
Add `imagePullPolicy: never` & test
Observe same failure

## Control Test #3 
# Create pod & container service directly from podman run cmds
podman pod create --replace \
    --name cloudctl \
    --hostname cloudctl \
    --publish 5000:5000/tcp \
    --dns-search codectl.io \
  --infra-image k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2

podman run -d --pod cloudctl --name registry \
    --volume /root/deploy/mirror:/root/deploy/mirror:z \
  docker.io/library/registry:latest

podman ps --all

# Observe normal startup of pod and container from local cache without erroneous api call
```

**Describe the results you received:**
Error:
```
+ podman play kube cloudctl.yml
Trying to pull docker.io/library/registry:latest...
  Get "https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/": dial tcp: lookup registry-1.docker.io on 162.150.8.51:53: dial udp 162.150.8.51:53: connect: network is unreachable
Error: unable to pull docker.io/library/registry:latest: Error initializing source docker://registry:latest: error pinging docker registry registry-1.docker.io: Get "https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/": dial tcp: lookup registry-1.docker.io on 162.150.8.51:53: dial udp 162.150.8.51:53: connect: network is unreachable

+ podman play kube cloudctl.yml
Trying to pull localhost/library/registry:latest...
  Get "https://localhost/v2/": dial tcp [::1]:443: connect: connection refused
Error: unable to pull localhost/library/registry:latest: Error initializing source docker://localhost/library/registry:latest: error pinging docker registry localhost: Get "https://localhost/v2/": dial tcp [::1]:443: connect: connection refused
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Expected `podman play kube` command to mirror the local cache image use capability of `podman run`
Expected `podman play kube` "disconnected" capability being used in production to be a stable behavior.
Expected `podman play kube` to not look for an api at `https://localhost/v2`

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
root@sparta:~# podman version
Version:      2.0.6
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.14.6
Built:        Tue Sep  1 12:26:51 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

      crun version 0.14.1
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-2.0.6-1.fc32.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**
Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Tested on:
  - Local libvirt Fedora32 VM
  - Physical fedora 32 server
  - RHEL 8 EC2 instance
  - CoreOS EC2 instance

comments.body:
Thanks for opening the issue, @usrbinkat!

@haircommander, do you have cycles to tackle the issue? Looks like the pull-policy is off.
I actually have some cycles now.

Got it. The reason for pulling the image is to be compatible with Kubernetes. Kubernetes [always pull an image](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#updating-images) if it has the `:latest` tag. If you wish to change that, you need to use a different tag.
I am going to close the issue but feel free to comment or re-open if I am mistaken.
